summary: This response incorrectly addresses a non-clinical query regarding hormonal contraception effects on cancer risk when the patient presents with adolescent acne requiring dermatological management. No clinically pertinent information was derived from retrieving data on ovarian cancer; thus, it fails to address the actual medical need related to treating persistent acne in adolescence through appropriate pharmacotherapy such as isotretinoin, spironolactone, or other anti-androgenic agents tailored for adolescents.
